title: Add schema validation to save_config_api endpoint
reasoning: BL-007 is the single highest-value task. It's a security improvement (matching anti-stagnation preference away from bug fixes), activates the dead validate_config() function, prevents corrupt config writes from causing 500 errors, and is a focused low-effort change across 2 files. Recent commits were exclusively bug fixes (zombie processes, thinking_delta suppression, web UI hash, f-string compat). BL-007 scores highest on Value=Impact/(Effort*Risk/10)=11.67, with effort=3 well under the 5 cap.
implementation_plan: In save_config_api(), call validate_config(data) before save_config(data)
Return HTTP 422 with structured validation errors on invalid input
Ensure partial config updates are supported (only validate keys present in the request body, not all required keys)
Add backward-compatible handling: empty/partial configs pass validation if no explicitly invalid values

update_existing.changes: priority=high → critical (activates dead code, prevents security issue + 500 errors)
status=completed (make lint-all + pytest exist in CI)
update description to note coverage is partial (156 tests across 7 modules, missing loop_manager/server/loop.py tests)
add evidence of diverging defaults between config_file.py (timeout=120, port=8000) and config_manager.py (timeout=600, no port)
add validate_config (config_manager.py) to dead code list alongside validate_json_output

add_new.title: Add mypy type-checking to CI pipeline + Makefile
Consolidate duplicate worker_term append logic in loop_manager._parse_daemon_line
Fix config_file.py/config_manager.py default port mismatch (8000 vs 8090)
Remove redundant pip install from make test target
Add Dependabot config for dependency updates
